title: Sustainability Content Experience
description: This project focused on exploring how sustainability content could become a strategic pillar of the digital experience by designing a dedicated Menu-tab with content for environmentally conscious consumers.
challenge: Create a scalable digital experience that integrates sustainability content into the existing ecosystem while strengthening brand perception and improving content discoverability.

sections.col1Content: Dedicated Menu-tab with sustainability content in the product website.
sections.col2Content: UX Research, Information Architecture, UX/UI Design, Design System, Usability Testing, Competitive Analysis

sections.col1Content: Product Owner, Tech Lead, Developers, Marketing Stakeholders
sections.col2Content: Create a scalable digital experience that integrates sustainability content into the existing ecosystem while strengthening brand perception and improving content discoverability.

sections.label: Integrating Sustainability into a laundry stain remover UK's site
sections.content: Research-driven UX strategy to define how sustainability content could become a meaningful part of a laundry stain remover UK's site.

sections.label: 1 · The Challenge
sections.content: How to incorporate sustainability content within a site focused on products and cleaning solutions without affecting the current experience or generating a perception of greenwashing? The company sought to explore new ways to communicate its sustainability commitment through its website, which was primarily focused on products, stain removal, washing tips, and fabric care.

sections.label: Business Problem
sections.intro: Sustainability was present in some company initiatives, but:
items: It was hard to discover.
It had no dedicated space.
The message was not actionable.
Information was scattered.
There was no clear content strategy.

items.title: Main
items.description: Identify opportunities to incorporate a new Sustainability section into the product website.

items.title: Benchmark
items.description: Understand how other brands communicate sustainability and identify successful patterns.

items.title: Content opportunities
items.description: Detect content gaps and propose a new information architecture.

items.title: Findability
items.description: Improve content discoverability across the site.

sections.label: Role · Senior UX Designer
sections.intro: Discovery, Strategy and Product Definition. Responsibilities included:
items: UX Research
Competitive Analysis & Benchmark
Heuristic Evaluation
Content Audit
Content Strategy
Information Architecture
Navigation Strategy
UX Recommendations
Opportunity definition
Content roadmap

sections.label: 2 · Methodology
sections.content: The research covered a current site audit (navigation, content, architecture, CTAs, discoverability, mobile experience) and a competitive benchmark across multiple industries: Laundry & Detergents (Ariel, Persil, Comfort), Home Care (Cif, Domestos), Personal Care (Dove, Gillette), Food & Beverage (Hellmann's, Knorr, PepsiCo), and Fashion & Lifestyle (Adidas, Levi's, Gucci).

sections.label: Research Questions
items: How do brands make sustainability visible?
What content patterns do they use?
What type of storytelling drives more engagement?
What level of depth do users expect?
How do they integrate sustainability without affecting the main experience?

sections.label: 3 · Key Findings
items: Most leading brands dedicate a specific navigation section to sustainability (Sustainability, Our Purpose, Planet & Society, Our Impact).
Successful brands transform sustainability into educational content: guides, tips, articles, tutorials, stories.
The best experiences show concrete and measurable actions: plastic reduction, recyclable packaging, emissions reduction, 2030 goals.
Users navigate between: Education > Advices > Products > Brand commitments > Actions.
The most successful brands make sustainability practical and actionable: how to recycle, donate, extend clothing life, save water, reduce energy consumption.

sections.label: Opportunity Detected
sections.content: The analysis revealed a clear opportunity: position the brand as an ally for extending the life of garments and fostering more sustainable consumption habits. This connects perfectly with the value proposition: 'Keep Clothes Alive Longer.'

sections.label: Strategic Recommendations
items: Incorporate a new Sustainability or Sustainability Tips tab — directly derived from benchmark patterns.
Replace main CTA 'Ver episodios' with 'Learn how to make your clothes last longer' or 'Discover sustainable laundry habits'.
Reframe newsletter subscription to: 'Join our community and receive sustainability tips'.
Refocus FAQs on brand care, clothing maintenance, and sustainable habits — less on corporate group information.

items.title: Actionable Content
items.description: Make every sustainability message something the user can act on.

items.title: Discoverability
items.description: Ensure sustainability content is easy to find and navigate.

items.title: Engagement
items.description: Increased time on site and interaction.

items.title: Purpose positioning
items.description: Align the product with global sustainability commitments.

sections.label: Key Learnings
items: Sustainability needs to be useful, not just visible.
Educational content builds more trust than institutional messaging.
The best experiences turn sustainability into everyday habits.
Cross-industry benchmarking can surface innovation opportunities that direct competitor analysis misses.
